featured.name: SafeAgent
featured.tagline: Exactly-once execution guard for AI agent side effects.
featured.why_radar: Every developer running AI agents in production has felt this pain. The moment your agent starts calling external APIs or executing commands, you need idempotency guarantees. SafeAgent solves the unglamorous but critical problem of making sure your agent doesn't accidentally charge your customer twice or send duplicate emails when things go wrong.

featured.name: Beam Protocol
featured.tagline: SMTP for AI agents with natural language agent-to-agent communication.
featured.why_radar: We're finally seeing the infrastructure layer for multi-agent systems emerge. Beam Protocol tackles the unsexy but essential problem of how agents actually talk to each other without breaking. Think of it as the plumbing that makes agent swarms possible rather than just a marketing buzzword.

picks.name: Beecon
picks.tagline: Infrastructure as Intent, open source IaC built for AI agents.
picks.why_radar: Instead of writing YAML configs, you tell Beecon what you want in plain English and it figures out the infrastructure. It's the logical next step from infrastructure as code to infrastructure as conversation. Early days, but this is where DevOps is heading.

picks.name: Agentic Context Engine
picks.tagline: LLM agents that write Python to analyse execution traces at scale.
picks.why_radar: Debugging distributed systems is hard enough without AI agents in the mix. This tool lets agents write their own analysis code to understand what went wrong in your traces. It's like having a junior engineer who never gets tired of digging through logs, except this one writes better Python.
